item.title: EU's MiCA regulation entered full force July 1, requiring all crypto firms serving EU customers to hold a MiCA license.
U.S. regulators face July 18 deadline to finalize implementation rules under the GENIUS Act stablecoin framework.
U.S. crypto market structure bill targets Senate floor vote the week of July 20 or 27, pending support from 7 Senate Democrats.
SEC plans to propose Regulation Crypto this month, creating temporary registration exemptions for digital asset offerings.
SEC and CFTC launched joint Project Crypto initiative to establish a shared asset taxonomy and eliminate duplicate compliance requirements.
UK's FCA unveiled a comprehensive crypto regulatory framework, winning early praise for its internationally connected approach.
SEC approved Grayscale's multi-asset ETP covering Bitcoin, Ether, XRP, Solana, and Cardano, its first multi-crypto U.S. product.
Grayscale activated in-kind staking on its U.S.-listed Ethereum and Solana ETPs, the first U.S. spot crypto products to offer staking.
Vanguard posted its first Head of Digital Assets role, signaling a shift by the $11T asset manager toward tokenization and crypto custody.
Stripe's stablecoin unit applied for a U.S. national bank trust charter to manage reserves and offer custody services.
Taiwan enacted crypto legislation giving licensed banks a regulatory first-mover advantage in stablecoin issuance and custody.
A coordinated attack drained 180,900 SOL (~$14.2M) from a single Solana-based wallet, one of the largest single-wallet exploits of 2026.
The Ill Bloom wallet seed-generation flaw was exploited for over $5M across 431 wallets in coordinated sweeps.
A vulnerability in the Aptos blockchain that could have put $70B in assets at systemic risk was quietly patched without losses.
Crypto exploits hit a record 70 incidents in Q2 2026 alone; total H1 2026 losses fell below $1 billion.
